Title: Climate change looks grim but we have many of the solutions
Event_Description: **11 June |** ***[Climate change looks grim but we have many of the solutions](https://iceds.anu.edu.au/news-events/events/climate-change-looks-grim-we-have-many-solutions)*** ATSE Oration 2026, delivered by Emeritus Professor Mark Howden, explores the realities of climate change impacts and discusses practical solutions.

Title: World Environment Day: The Materiality of Waste
Event_Description: **6 June |** ***[World Environment Day: The Materiality of Waste](https://www.unaansw.org.au/donate)*** is supported by the United Nations Association of Australia (NSW Division), focusing on waste and sustainability.

Title: Review your energy bills: one-on-one help
Event_Description: **13 June |** ***[Review your energy bills: one-on-one help](https://www.eventbrite.com.au/e/review-your-energy-bills-one-on-one-help-tickets-1989963729082?aff=ebdssbdestsearch)*** is in-person one-on-one support organised by Gisborne Library, bringing your bill for expert advice on the most cost-effective energy plan.

Title: The MAC Band -Fleetwood Mac Experience @ West Beach SLSC
Event_Description: **13 June |** ***[The MAC Band -Fleetwood Mac Experience @ West Beach SLSC](https://www.eventbrite.com.au/e/the-mac-band-fleetwood-mac-experience-west-beach-slsc-tickets-1988660696677?aff=ebdssbdestsearch)*** is an in-person Fleetwood Mac tribute show by The MAC Band Fleetwood Mac Experience at West Beach Surf Life Saving Club, 8–11pm.

Title: From Strategies to Action: Aligning Climate Goals, Development and Finance
Event_Description: **11 June |** ***[From Strategies to Action: Aligning Climate Goals, Development and Finance](https://hub.wri.org/events/2026/6/strategies-action-aligning-climate-goals-development-and-finance?returnTo=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.wri.org%2Fevents)*** is co-organized by Global Green Growth Institute (GGGI) and World Resources Institute (WRI), focusing on aligning climate planning with economic realities and mobilizing climate finance.

Title: Governance and Risk Management Forum 2026 - VIC (Day 1)
Event_Description: **10 June |** ***[Governance and Risk Management Forum 2026 - VIC (Day 1)](https://www.governanceinstitute.com.au/events/e09672-governance-and-risk-management-forum-2026-vic-day-1/)*** by the Governance Institute, focusing on governance excellence and building resilience.

Title: Governance and Risk Management Forum 2026 - VIC
Event_Description: **10 June |** ***[Governance and Risk Management Forum 2026 - VIC](https://www.governanceinstitute.com.au/events/e09671-governance-and-risk-management-forum-2026-vic/)*** is hosted by the Governance Institute of Australia, focusing on governance excellence and embedding resilience through risk management and organisational resilience.

Title: Governance and Risk Management Forum 2026 - VIC (Day 2)
Event_Description: **11 June |** ***[Governance and Risk Management Forum 2026 - VIC (Day 2)](https://www.governanceinstitute.com.au/events/e09673-governance-and-risk-management-forum-2026-vic-day-2/)*** from the Governance Institute of Australia focuses on embedding organisational resilience in risk management, culture and frameworks.

Title: First Nations Director Update 2026: Online

Title: Canberra Directors Download - External Party Risk
Event_Description: **10 June |** ***[Canberra Directors Download - External Party Risk](https://www.aicd.com.au/events/all-events/act/EVNT-001078.html)*** by the AICD invites members to explore evolving external party risk and directors’ oversight beyond the boardroom.

Title: National Economic Update 2026

Title: VIC Womens Lunch 2026
Event_Description: **12 June |** ***[VIC Womens Lunch 2026](https://www.aicd.com.au/events/all-events/vic/EVNT-001085.html)*** by AICD, featuring Launa Inman as Chair of the PayPal Melbourne Fashion Festival for a Victorian women’s lunch discussion.

Title: Innovation and Technological Change for Industrial Transition and Regional Development
Event_Description: **10 June |** ***[Innovation and Technological Change for Industrial Transition and Regional Development](https://www.oecd.org/en/events/2026/06/innovation-and-technological-change-for-industrial-transition-and-regional-development.html)***—organised by the OECD and hosted by the Government of Québec as a hybrid seminar/webinar on regional innovation and technological transition.

Title: Summer School on AI+Data: Practice & Law
Event_Description: **10 June |** ***[Summer School on AI+Data: Practice & Law](https://www.oecd.org/en/events/2026/06/summer-school-on-ai-data-practice-and-law.html)*** is organised by FBK’s Ethics & Legal Innovation Lab with the OECD Trento Centre, offering practical training on EU data and AI regulations.

Title: The OECD Productivity Manual at 25: Looking back, taking stock, and looking ahead
Event_Description: **11 June |** ***[The OECD Productivity Manual at 25: Looking back, taking stock, and looking ahead](https://www.oecd.org/en/events/2026/06/the-oecd-productivity-manual-at-25-looking-back-taking-stock-and-looking-ahead.html)*** is a hybrid workshop organised by the OECD Statistics and Data Directorate (SDD) and OECD STI, revisiting productivity measurement and planning the Manual update.

Title: EDA Annual Convention 2026

Title: WEBINAR | 10 June: Sustainability reporting & climate-related disclosures update
Event_Description: **10 June |** ***[WEBINAR | 10 June: Sustainability reporting & climate-related disclosures update](https://www.a2ep.org.au/post/webinar-10-june-sustainability-reporting-climate-related-disclosures-update)*** is a Zoom webinar with Lisa Zembrodt and Michael Cox (Schneider Electric), Jodi Litzenberger (EnergyLink Services) and Alison Atherton (UTS), covering sustainability reporting and climate-related disclosure updates.

Title: Inside Bonn Climate Change Conference 2026 Climate negotiators return to Bonn for the 64th sessions of the Subsidiary Bodies of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) (SB 64), the first major session since the 30th United Nations Climate Change Conference (COP 30).  Event Hub June 8, 2026
Event_Description: **8 June |** ***[Inside Bonn Climate Change Conference 2026 Climate negotiators return to Bonn for the 64th sessions of the Subsidiary Bodies of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) (SB 64), the first major session since the 30th United Nations Climate Change Conference (COP 30).  Event Hub June 8, 2026](https://www.iisd.org/events/inside-bonn-climate-change-conference-2026)*** welcomes IISD coverage of SB 64 UNFCCC climate negotiations in Bonn, Germany.

Title: SB 64 Press Conference | Santa Marta to Bonn—Building Political Weight for the Fossil Fuel Transition This SB 64 press conference in Bonn will reflect on how the momentum from Santa Marta can build political weight for the transition away from fossil fuels within the formal UNFCCC process.  Conference June 8, 2026
Event_Description: **8 June |** ***[SB 64 Press Conference | Santa Marta to Bonn—Building Political Weight for the Fossil Fuel Transition This SB 64 press conference in Bonn will reflect on how the momentum from Santa Marta can build political weight for the transition away from fossil fuels within the formal UNFCCC process.  Conference June 8, 2026](https://www.iisd.org/events/sb-64-press-conference-santa-marta-bonn-building-political-weight-fossil-fuel-transition)***, an IISD open-to-public press conference (with livestream) on building political momentum for the fossil-fuel transition in UNFCCC negotiations.

Title: Student Workshop: Inclusive and Sustainable Growth Beyond GDP in Zambia Students will confront the limitations of GDP and explore alternative and complementary measures to GDP and their policy implications for Zambia.  Workshop June 9, 2026
Event_Description: **9 June |** ***[Student Workshop: Inclusive and Sustainable Growth Beyond GDP in Zambia Students will confront the limitations of GDP and explore alternative and complementary measures to GDP and their policy implications for Zambia.  Workshop June 9, 2026](https://www.iisd.org/events/student-workshop-inclusive-and-sustainable-growth-beyond-gdp-zambia)*** is hosted by the International Institute for Sustainable Development (IISD) and IDRC, inviting students to explore “beyond GDP” measures and their policy implications for Zambia.

Title: Policy Workshop: Inclusive and Sustainable Growth Beyond GDP in Zambia Policymakers will explore core concepts, global approaches, and key components of "beyond GDP" and assess their relevance for Zambia.  Workshop June 10, 2026
Event_Description: **10 June |** Join ***[Policy Workshop: Inclusive and Sustainable Growth Beyond GDP in Zambia Policymakers will explore core concepts, global approaches, and key components of "beyond GDP" and assess their relevance for Zambia.  Workshop June 10, 2026](https://www.iisd.org/events/policy-workshop-inclusive-and-sustainable-growth-beyond-gdp-zambia)*** by IISD and UNCTAD (by invitation) to discuss beyond-GDP measurement and policy implications for Zambia.
